assertions.text: V1 was traveling east in the first of four lanes on a positively divided, two-way roadway.
V2 was traveling east in the same lane ahead of V1.
The front of V1 contacted the back of V2 (V1 rear-ended V2).
V2 was redirected off the right side of the road after contact.
The right side of V2 contacted the concrete traffic barrier.
Both vehicles then came to rest in the road after V2 contacted the barrier.
At final rest, V2 had a major fire.
There was at least one impact: V1's front contacting V2's rear; V2 then contacting the concrete barrier may be a second distinct impact.
V2 experienced a major fire indicating severe post-impact consequences.

verdicts.evidence: Vehicle telemetry shows pre-crash speed samples of [253.0](T2) km/h at t=-5.0 s and [203.0](T2) km/h at t=-0.5 s, but the data do not include heading or lane information, so travel direction and lane count cannot be determined from sensors alone.

Telemetry records speed samples only: [253.0](T2) km/h first and [203.0](T2) km/h last before impact; there is no information about other road users (V2) or their position in the same lane in the available channels.

EDR crash-pulse shows a large cumulative longitudinal delta-V peaking at [−89.0](T2) km/h around t=[0.14](T1) s with a final longitudinal delta-V of about [−86.0](T2) km/h, consistent with a high-energy forward-impact (V1 front contacting V2 rear).

Telemetry records a cumulative lateral delta-V peaking at [−23.0](T2) km/h at t=[0.12](T2) s, but sensor axes are device-frame and do not indicate road-relative direction (right/left); thus redirection off the right side of the road is unverifiable.

There is no sensor channel indicating contact between V2 and a concrete barrier; the vehicle telemetry for V1 shows lateral delta-V [−23.0](T2) km/h but does not identify which side of V2 contacted a barrier.

EDR summary shows one recorded event [num_events_recorded=1](T1) and crash-pulse ends by t≈[0.3](T1) s, but there is no clear final-rest positions or sequence timestamps for both vehicles in the available telemetry, so the post-impact resting sequence is unverifiable.
No fire-detection channel or post-crash imaging is available in the telemetry; the EDR/telemetry contains no information about a major fire at V2's final rest.

EDR/CRASH data indicate a single recorded crash event [num_events_recorded=1](T1) with a large longitudinal delta-V peaking at [−89.0](T2) km/h, consistent with at least one impact (V1 front into V2 rear); telemetry does not confirm a separate second impact as a distinct crash event.

Telemetry shows high impact severity for V1 with longitudinal delta-V peak [−89.0](T2) km/h, but there are no sensor channels reporting fire severity on V2; linking fire magnitude to telemetry is unverifiable.

result.rationale: Sensor data from V1 provide strong evidence of a high-energy rear-end impact (large longitudinal delta-V: [−89.0](T2) km/h at t=[0.14](T2) s) but lack positional, heading, and external-vehicle or fire-status channels. Many narrative details about lanes, directions, V2 behavior, barrier contact side, and post-crash fire are not recorded in the available telemetry.

result.time_zero: impact (t=0); pre-crash negative, crash pulse positive

channels.name: delta_v_long
channels.desc: Cumulative longitudinal delta-V during impact (times converted from ms to s)

channels.name: delta_v_lat
channels.desc: Cumulative lateral delta-V during impact (times converted from ms to s)

result.notes: NHTSA CISS EDR data: sparse pre-crash series (~2 Hz, t<0) plus a crash-pulse delta-V curve (t>=0, ~10 ms steps). t=0 is the impact.

delta_v_long.note: cumulative velocity change during the crash pulse

delta_v_lat.note: cumulative velocity change during the crash pulse
